methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Type One Energy is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by John Canik, currently valued at $200M+ post-money with $100M+ in total capital raised. The nuclear energy sector includes a comp universe of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier to Type One's $200M last post-money valuation, yielding $380M. This multiplier reflects typical Series B to next-stage step-up dynamics. The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints—while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtered sets by stage and valuation tier contain fewer than 3 comps, making narrow peer benchmarking unreliable without resorting to misleadingly broad all-sector medians.

Given data constraints, the triangulated valuation range is $323M to $437M, with a median of $380M, supported by a single quantitative method. Key uncertainties include technology validation, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital intensity through commercialization. The wide range ($114M spread) reflects methodological limitations rather than underlying business conviction. Additional private-market data on development-stage nuclear peers would narrow this range and improve confidence in the valuation.
